title: Calonema polychromum
taxonomy: Calonema polychromum is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This flowering plant is known for its vibrant colors and unique growth patterns. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Calonema polychromum is closely related to other orchid species and shares many characteristics with them. The classification of Calonema polychromum is further divided into the genus Calonema and the subfamily Epidendroideae. The specific classification of Calonema polychromum is still under review by taxonomists.

intro: Calonema polychromum, also known as Himalayan calonema, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calonema polychromum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Calonema polychromum is often made into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
